milestoneTitle: M10.11 — Accent retune: parallel per-section token families (Learn green / Projects blue / Tools orange). Retires the unified --base → --accent-* derivation after diagnosing a WebKit quirk: a custom property whose value wraps another custom property in color-mix() does NOT lazily re-resolve per descendant when an ancestor overrides the referenced variable — the inner var() freezes at :root's value and inherits as a frozen color. Symptom: `--base: red` on the Projects panel turned the panel outline red (proving the override applied) but CTA border, icon stroke, and glow inside the panel all stayed green. Fix: three parallel families at :root (`--learn-*`, `--projects-*`, `--tools-*`), each a literal color-mix triad with no var() inside the token definitions. Section / panel / chip scopes then assign --accent-* to their matching family via a single var() reference — the one-level indirection WebKit has always resolved correctly. Component consumers unchanged (~80 rules still read --accent-solid / border / glow / surface / warm / hover / soft). Three stops per family calibrated 78 / 72 / 45 via color-mix(in oklch, base, black) for the dark legibility stop and color-mix(in oklch, base, tint-target) for the modest + heavy yellow-tinted stops. Projects biases tint-target to amber (#f0c23a) not pure yellow, because blue + yellow in oklch interpolates through green and would collide visually with Learn. Home section gets no --accent-* reassignment so the Dashboard reads greyscale at rest; color only enters when a panel activates (which re-scopes via .dash-panel:has(...)). Bundles a small folder-icon widening on the Dashboard Projects panel — body right edge extended in the viewBox so the silhouette reads more folder-like than square. New /accent-retune skill added at .claude/skills/accent-retune/ encoding the parallel-families architecture, the WebKit pitfall, the 3-stop recipe, all six scopes that must update in lockstep, and the Projects amber-tint caveat — so future retunes are one invocation instead of a hand-edit sweep.
